images.title: Edge of Ascent
images.gear: OLYMPUS CORPORATION E-M1MarkIII, M.100-400mm F5.0-6.3 + MC-20
images.field_notes: As the Egret departs, it feels like it is falling. Although it does briefly descend, this is all done in order for it to take to the sky.

images.title: Friend
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.field_notes: As the kayakers moved through the bay, this seal peaked up to check on them.

images.title: Edge of Ascent
images.gear: OLYMPUS CORPORATION E-M1MarkIII, M.100-400mm F5.0-6.3 + MC-20
images.field_notes: As the Egret departs, it feels like it is falling. Although it does briefly descend, this is all done in order for it to take to the sky.

images.title: Herb
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OLYMPUS M.150-400mm F4.5
images.field_notes: Herb. North Atlantic Right Whale #1250. A 40 year veteran of the bay of Fundy, seeing one of these rare creatures in the wild will alter you perception for the rest of your life.

images.title: Lizard
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 100-400mm F5.0-6.3
images.field_notes: Seen on the salt flats, the texture of the lizard’s skin begged for a photo.

images.title: Black Vulture
images.gear: OLYMPUS CORPORATION E-M1MarkIII, M.100-400mm F5.0-6.3 + MC-20
images.field_notes: I've always been fascinated with Black Vultures. While many species shy away from human presence, the vulture is remarkably bold. It doesn't skirt the edges of our world, but fully ocupies it. Their ancient asthetic, and complex social bonds are a reminder nature doesn't always have to be delicate to be magnificent.

images.title: Hidden Prince
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.field_notes: A dinosaur of a bird. Listening to thousands of them at the same time is an incredible noise that stays with you.

images.title: Dinner, Alone
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 100-400mm F5.0-6.3
images.field_notes: Seeing Ospreys always excites. They are probably my favorite raptor, even if common around the world. This one had found its dinner and was taking it somewhere more private to eat.

images.title: Line of Flight
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 100-400mm F5.0-6.3
images.field_notes: As flamingos flew all around us, this one happened to line up perfectly with the layers in the mountains behind.

images.title: The Greatest Egret
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.field_notes: A beautiful capture of the aigrettes, the fine ornamental feathers, with the sun highlighting them perfect.

images.title: Out of the Surf
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OM 100-400mm F5.0-6.3
images.field_notes: The Oystercatchers were out this day all around us, with this particular one playing by the crashing waves.

images.title: Mountain Bluebird
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.field_notes: The electric blue plumage of the Western Bluebird is always striking to see, especially in contrast to the neutrals of the dunes.

images.title: Screech Owl
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC + MC-14
images.location: The Parklands of Floyds Fork, KY
images.field_notes: This particular fellow stands guard over the bridge. Everyday hundreds of people pass by, none the wiser to his presence.

images.title: Shared Waters
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 + MC-14
images.field_notes: Watching Ospreys hunt is always incredible. Seeing them show up their human counterparts right in front of you is a bonus.

images.title: Swallow Swallowing a Swallow
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC + MC-14
images.field_notes: Watching the swallows play by the pond, one was spotted still on the branches in the middle. After focusing in on it, the mother came by to feed her young in the right place, right time scenario.

images.title: Ibis
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 + MC-14
images.field_notes: When a vagrant comes to town, you visit. This one approached within inches after lying in the mud waiting for the right shot.

images.title: Baltimore Oriole
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1MarkII, OM 150-400mm F4.5 TC
images.field_notes: The bright colors of the Baltimore Oriole is always such a delight. The perfect contrast from the orange of the feathers to the blue sky is perfect.

images.title: Born pt. 1
images.gear: OM Digital Solutions OM-1, OLYMPUS M.150-400mm F4.5
images.field_notes: On this particularly cold day the birds came out after a long night of snow. This White-throated Sparrow shows how it stays warm by poofing up.
